skill.name: project-planning
skill.description: Generate project planning documents from a project concept, including a project vision, architecture decision record, technical specification, and development roadmap.
skill.summary: Create structured planning documents for AI-assisted software projects.
skill.author: ByronWilliamsCPA

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high and medium patterns, but manual review found the high cryptography, path traversal, and Ruby backtick findings are false positives from Markdown documentation and templates. The remaining real risks are low: the skill reads and writes local planning documents, includes a local validation script, and documents optional git workflow commands. No evidence found of prompt injection, network exfiltration, secret harvesting, destructive file operations, or remote code execution.

low_findings.title: Local Planning Document File Access
low_findings.description: The skill is designed to read templates and create or validate files under docs/planning. This is expected for a documentation skill, but users should review generated documents before committing them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicit and local to planning documents. I found no network transfer, secret collection, or broad filesystem traversal behavior.

low_findings.title: Workflow Contains Local Shell and Git Command Examples
low_findings.description: The synthesize workflow documents local validation commands and optional git branch creation. The workflow frontmatter limits Bash to git commands, and the examples do not exfiltrate data or execute remote code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are visible and limited to checking local files or creating a git branch. Risk remains low because command execution is constrained and project-local.

dangerous_patterns.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Ruby Shell Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The static analyzer flagged many Markdown inline-code and fenced-code delimiters as Ruby backtick execution. These files are documentation and templates, not Ruby source code, and no evidence of dynamic command execution was found in those locations.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown examples, tables, or template blocks. The only executable source file is Python and it does not use shell execution APIs.

dangerous_patterns.title: Planning Terms Misclassified as Weak Cryptography
dangerous_patterns.description: The high-severity weak cryptography detections appear to come from terms such as ADR, MD, IDs, and placeholder text in documentation templates. No evidence was found of cryptographic primitives, password hashing, encryption code, or security-sensitive digest use.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found documentation placeholders and planning terminology, not implemented cryptographic algorithms. The Python script imports only re, sys, and pathlib.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relative Link Misclassified as Path Traversal
dangerous_patterns.description: The path traversal warning points to a Markdown reference from an ADR template to a sibling planning document. It is a documentation link, not code that resolves user-controlled paths.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The cited sequence appears inside a fenced Markdown template link. There is no file operation tied to that relative path.

dangerous_patterns.title: Local Planning Document File Access
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill is designed to read templates and create or validate files under docs/planning. This is expected for a documentation skill, but users should review generated documents before committing them.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The file access is explicit and local to planning documents. I found no network transfer, secret collection, or broad filesystem traversal behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Workflow Contains Local Shell and Git Command Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: The synthesize workflow documents local validation commands and optional git branch creation. The workflow frontmatter limits Bash to git commands, and the examples do not exfiltrate data or execute remote code.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The shell examples are visible and limited to checking local files or creating a git branch. Risk remains low because command execution is constrained and project-local.

content.user_title: Plan Software Projects with Structured Docs
content.value_statement: New software projects often lose context before implementation starts. This skill turns a project concept into aligned vision, ADR, technical spec, and roadmap documents.
actual_capabilities: Generates a Project Vision and Scope document from a project concept.
Creates architecture decision records for important technical choices.
Builds a technical implementation specification with stack, architecture, data model, and security sections.
Produces a phased development roadmap with milestones, tasks, dependencies, and success criteria.
Validates generated planning documents for placeholders, required sections, length, and local cross-references.
Synthesizes planning documents into a PROJECT-PLAN.md workflow with phase branch guidance.
limitations: It cannot guarantee the generated plan is technically correct without human review.
Consensus review depends on the optional zen-mcp-server tool being available.
The validation script checks document structure, not implementation quality.
Roadmap estimates are planning aids and may need adjustment after discovery work.

use_cases.title: Start a New Software Project
use_cases.description: Turn an idea into a compact planning pack before writing code or opening issues.

use_cases.title: Align Architecture Before Implementation
use_cases.description: Document key choices, constraints, and implementation scope before assigning development work.

use_cases.title: Prepare AI Coding Context
use_cases.description: Create focused documents that Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can reference during later coding sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Create Initial Planning Docs
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner prompt for a new project idea.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use project-planning to create planning documents for this project: [describe users, problem, platform, and constraints].

prompt_templates.title: Generate Docs from Existing Repository
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a repository already has structure or technology choices.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the current repository structure and create project planning documents that match the existing stack and constraints.

prompt_templates.title: Refine Architecture Decisions
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the first draft needs stronger technical rationale.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the generated ADRs and improve the rationale, alternatives, consequences, and links to the technical specification.

prompt_templates.title: Synthesize an Execution Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced prompt after all planning documents exist.
prompt_templates.prompt: Synthesize the planning documents into PROJECT-PLAN.md, map roadmap phases to branch names, and create a Phase 0 task list.

output_examples.input: A CLI app that helps freelancers track invoices, payments, and late follow-ups.
output: Project Vision and Scope defining target users, core invoice workflows, and measurable MVP goals.
ADR for local SQLite storage with trade-offs and migration considerations.
Technical specification covering Python, Click, data model, commands, validation, and error handling.
Roadmap with foundation, MVP billing workflows, reporting, and release polish phases.

output_examples.input: A small internal web tool for reviewing support ticket trends each week.
output: Vision document describing analysts, support managers, core reports, and out-of-scope analytics.
ADR explaining framework and data storage choices.
Tech spec with components, API endpoints, security notes, and performance targets.
Roadmap with milestones for ingestion, dashboards, permissions, testing, and documentation.

output_examples.input: Existing planning docs are generated. Create a single plan for implementation.
output: PROJECT-PLAN.md with executive summary, scope, branch strategy, phases, risks, dependencies, and next steps.
Phase 0 task list that connects roadmap deliverables to concrete setup work.

best_practices: Provide the project goal, users, constraints, and preferred technology stack in the first prompt.
Review assumptions, timelines, and ADR trade-offs before using the plan for implementation.
Update planning documents when architecture, scope, or roadmap decisions change.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated planning documents as final requirements without stakeholder review.
Do not ask for broad implementation before the vision, ADR, technical spec, and roadmap agree.
Do not leave placeholder values in planning documents before committing them.

faq.question: What documents does this skill generate?
faq.answer: It generates a project vision, architecture decision record, technical specification, and development roadmap.

faq.question: Can it work with an existing codebase?
faq.answer: Yes. It can incorporate repository structure and known project constraints into the planning documents.

faq.question: Does it require external services?
faq.answer: No for basic generation. Optional consensus review requires the zen-mcp-server consensus tool.

faq.question: Does the validation script modify files?
faq.answer: No. The script reads local planning documents and reports missing sections, placeholders, length issues, and broken local links.

faq.question: Is it suitable for non-software projects?
faq.answer: It is designed for software development planning and assumes technical specs, ADRs, and implementation roadmaps.

faq.question: How should teams use the generated roadmap?
faq.answer: Treat it as a starting plan. Review dependencies, estimates, and success criteria before assigning work.
